title: Discussion on the proposal for the city's polycentric plan
description: Work session discussion regarding a proposed polycentric plan for the city, which would guide future growth and development patterns
notes: Polycentric planning typically involves identifying multiple centers of development rather than single downtown focus; significant strategic planning implication for future rezones and subdivisions

title: Discussion on proposed amendments to the city's municipal code
description: Work session discussion on proposed amendments to American Fork City municipal code
notes: Specific amendments not detailed in agenda; could affect zoning, development standards, or land use regulations city-wide

title: Amendment and recodification of Title 15 and 17 of the American Fork Municipal Code
description: Public hearing on amendment and recodification of Title 15 and 17 of the American Fork Municipal Code to recodify and identify existing conditions, planning and visioning process, zoning, preferred development objectives, preferred development scenario, and implementation strategies.
notes: Comprehensive municipal code update affecting Title 15 and 17. Documents available in Planning Commission Packet at www.AmericanFork.gov Agenda Center.

title: Update to the American Fork General Plan
description: Public hearing on an update to the American Fork General Plan to update all sections and establish an updated vision for growth and development in the City.
notes: Comprehensive General Plan update affecting all sections. Hearing scheduled for February 4, 2026 at 6:30 PM at American Fork City Hall, 31 N. Church Street. Documents available at www.AmericanFork.gov Agenda Center.

title: Adoption and Amendment of Design Standards and Public Improvement Specifications
description: American Fork City Planning Commission will consider adoption and amendment of Design Standards and Public Improvement Specifications applicable to development within the city. The proposal includes removing existing engineering standards from the Municipal Code and adopting new standards by reference, aligned with Utah APWA Standard Specifications while establishing uniform City-specific design criteria.
notes: Infrastructure standards update affecting all future development in the city. Planning Commission will make recommendation to City Council.

title: Jasper Lot Land Use Map Amendment at 570 E Main Street

location: 570 E Main Street, American Fork City
notes: Small parcel rezoning from residential to commercial use on Main Street corridor. Planning Commission will receive public comment and make recommendation.

title: Walton Lot Amended Final Plat at 794 W 1000 N Circle
description: Review and action on an application for an Amended Final Plat known as Walton Lot to allow for the building of a single-family home on one of the lots. The application will clean up a prior subdivision and ensure all lots meet underlying zone requirements.
location: 794 W 1000 N Circle, American Fork City
notes: Amended final plat application to remedy prior subdivision issues. Single-family residential development. Public hearing scheduled for March 2, 2026 at 9:00 AM in Public Works building at 275 East 200 North.

title: 04.28.26 Work Session agenda
agenda_text:
AMERICAN FORK CITY COUNCIL
APRIL 28, 2026
		WORK SESSION AGENDA




*Notice of Electronic Meeting*
One or more City Council members may be physically absent from this meeting but may participate electronically.

Notice is hereby given that the American Fork City Council will hold a work session on Tuesday, April 28, 2026, in the City Administration Conference Room, located at 51 East Main Street, commencing at 4:00 p.m. The agenda shall be as follows:
WORK SESSION
The purpose of the City Work Sessions is to prepare the City Council for upcoming agenda items on future City Council Meetings. The Work Session is not an action item meeting. No one attending the meeting should rely on any discussion or any perceived consensus as action or authorization. These come only from the City Council Meeting.

1.	Discussion on the fiscal year 2027 General Fund, Capital Projects, Fleet Fund, and Fitness Center fund.
2.	Training in Ethics and Conflict of Interest.
3.	Adjourn. 

Dated the 23rd day of April 2026.

/s/Terilyn Lurker
City Recorder









-	In accordance with the Americans with Disabilities Act, the City of American Fork will make reasonable accommodations to participate in the meeting. Requests for assistance can be made by contacting the City Recorder at 801-763-3000 at least 48 hours in advance of the meeting.
-	The order of agenda items may be changed to accommodate the needs of the City Council, staff, and the public.

title: Williams Development Agreement for natural gas power facility on Stadion parcel to support Meta data center expansion
description: Williams, an independent power producer, seeks a development agreement to co-locate a natural gas power facility on the Stadion property (zoned RTI) as an accessory use to the Meta data center expansion. The agreement clarifies rights and responsibilities between Williams and the City. Williams requests exceptions to city code including: (1) sound level allowance up to 75 dB (exceeding the 65 dB nighttime limit but consistent with neighboring industrial standards), and (2) permission to use above-ground power distribution lines. Williams is responsible for installing necessary public infrastructure.
notes: This is an accessory use to the Meta data center expansion. Natural gas power is a permitted use under the existing Stadion development agreement, but this new agreement provides additional clarification and seeks code exceptions for noise (75 dB) and above-ground utilities. Related to major data center infrastructure investment in Eagle Mountain.

title: Williams Development Agreement - Natural gas power facility co-location on Stadion parcel for Meta data center expansion
description: Williams, an independent power producer, seeks to co-locate a natural gas power facility on the Stadion property (zoned RTI) as an accessory use to facilitate Meta data center expansion. Development agreement negotiated to clarify rights and responsibilities. Williams requests code exceptions including: increased sound limit to 75 dB (exceeding city nighttime limit of 65 dB but consistent with industrial standards) and permission for above-ground power distribution lines. Williams responsible for installing necessary public infrastructure.
notes: Accessory use to Meta data center expansion. Natural gas power is permitted accessory use per existing Stadion development agreement. Requests exceptions: 75 dB sound limit (10 dB above city nighttime standard) and above-ground power distribution lines. Requires public hearing and action item approval.

title: Erda City Council Emergency Meeting
agenda_text:
Notice is hereby given that the Erda City Council will hold an emergency meeting on Friday March 27th, 2026 at 6:30 PM hold a closed session pursuant to Utah Code §52-4-205(1) for a strategy session to discuss imminent litigation requiring immediate council direction. The meeting will be held at the Erda City Fire Station/Office, located at 2163 West Erda Way, Erda, Utah


Call to Order
Roll Call
Motion and Vote to Enter Closed Session (reason: imminent litigation requiring immediate council direction)
Conduct Closed Session
Adjournment 


**All handouts for this meeting can be found on the city's website erda.gov under the calendar section**

Board may elect to go into a closed session which will not be open to the public pursuant to Utah Code Ann. §52-4-205(1).
**Format and Verbiage to be used when going into a closed session
'I move that we temporarily recess the Council meeting to go into a closed meeting for a strategy session to discuss pending or reasonably imminent litigation.'
 
The motion needs a second and a roll call vote.
 
The Chair then makes this statement:   'The motion passes and the Council will go into closed meeting for a strategy session to discuss pending or reasonably imminent litigation that will be held in this room.'


Pursuant to the Americans with Disabilities Act, Individuals needing special accommodations should contact Larry Kimpel at 435-354-9216

title: Public Hearing Notice for the Lehi City Community Development Block Grant
agenda_text:
Public Hearing and Public Comment Period Notice
Lehi City Community Development Block Grant
Mountainland Association of Governments will hold a public hearing to consider feedback on the Lehi City CDBG Annual Action Plan, which outlines community needs, potential community funding priorities, and program structure and governance for the program year 2026-2027. The amount of CDBG funds for this program year will be discussed along with the range of projects eligible.
The hearing will be held in the Lehi Council Chambers located at 131 North 100 East, Lehi, Utah, on April 28th at 7:00 pm. Suggestions will be solicited verbally, electronically, and in writing from all interested parties during the public comment period from April 3 to May 5, 2026. To comment or for further information, contact Claudia Saldana at 801-229-3673 or csaldana@mountainland.org.
The public hearing facility is fully accessible to persons with disabilities. Individuals needing special accommodations (including auxiliary aids/services or translation) during the hearing should notify Claudia Saldana at least five days prior to the hearing. A Utah Relay telephone number or a language interpreter can be provided upon request.
________________________________________
Mountainland Association of Governments llevará a cabo una audiencia pública para considerar la retroalimentación sobre el Plan de Acción Anual de la Ciudad de Lehi CDBG, que resume las necesidades de la comunidad, posibles prioridades de financiación comunitaria y la estructura y gestión del programa para el año 2026-2027. El monto estimado de los fondos CDBG para este año del programa se examinará junto con la gama de proyectos elegibles.
La audiencia se llevará a cabo en las oficinas de la ciudad de Lehi, 131 North 100 East, Lehi, Utah, el Martes 28 de Abril de 2026 a las 7:00 pm. Las sugerencias serán solicitadas verbalmente, electrónicamente y por escrito de todas las partes interesadas durante el período de comentarios públi

title: Notice of Quorum
agenda_text:
NOTICE OF POSSIBLE QUORUM

Notice is given that a quorum of Saratoga Springs City Council Members may be present at a social gathering for elected leaders with neighboring cities at Cedar Fort Town, Utah,

title: Public Notice- Approving Budget Amendments for FY 2025-2026
agenda_text:
Public Notice

Notice is hereby given that the Tooele Council of Tooele City, Utah, will hold a Public Hearing to consider approving budget amendments for fiscal year 2025-2026. The hearing will be held on Wednesday, March 4, 2026, at 7:00 p.m. The meeting will be held at the Tooele City Hall Council Chambers, located at 90 North Main Street, Tooele, Utah.

Tooele City public meetings may be recorded and transcribed for documentation and quality assurance purposes. By attending this meeting, you consent to being recorded. If you do not consent, we encourage you to join the City Council meeting electronically by visiting the Tooele City YouTube Channel, at https://www.youtube.com/@tooelecity or by going to YouTube.com and searching 'Tooele City Channel'. If you are attending electronically and would like to submit a written comment for the public comment period or for a public hearing item, please email cmpubliccomment@tooelecity.gov. If submission by email is not an option, written comments may be submitted to the City Recorder. Written comments must be submitted no later than the day prior to the meeting. Written comments will be addressed at the designated points in the meeting.

Pursuant to the Americans with Disabilities Act, Individuals Needing Special Accommodations Should Notify Shilo Baker, Tooele City Recorder, at 435-843-2113 or shilob@tooelecity.gov, Prior to the Meeting.

March 17, 2026, City Council Agenda
NOTICE OF A REGULAR CITY COUNCIL MEETING
March 17, 2026, at 12:00 PM
________________
PUBLIC NOTICE is hereby given that the Vineyard City Council will hold a regularly scheduled City Council meeting on Tuesday, March 17, 2026, at 12:00 PM, in the City Council Chambers at City Hall, 125 South Main Street, Vineyard, UT. This meeting can also be viewed on our live stream page.
AGENDA
Presiding Mayor Zack Stratton

1. CALL TO ORDER/INVOCATION/INSPIRATIONAL THOUGHT/PLEDGE OF ALLEGIANCE

2. PUBLIC COMMENTS
'Public Comments' is defined as time set aside for citizens to express their views for items not on the agenda. During a period designated for public comment, the mayor or chair may allot each speaker a maximum amount of time to present their comments, subject to extension by the mayor or by a majority vote of the council. Speakers offering duplicate comments may be limited. Because of the need for proper public notice, immediate action cannot be taken in the Council Meeting. The Chair of the meeting reserves the right to organize public comments by topic and may group speakers accordingly. If action is necessary, the item will be listed on a future agenda; however, the Council may elect to discuss the item if it is an immediate matter of concern. Public comments can be submitted ahead of time to tonyl@vineyardutah.gov.

title: Public Hearing for the Consolidated Fee Schedule
agenda_text:
PUBLIC NOTICE OF PUBLIC HEARING
VINEYARD CITY COUNCIL

Notice is hereby given that the Vineyard City Council will hold a Public Hearing on Tuesday, April 28, 2026, at 6:00 p.m., at Vineyard City Hall, 125 South Main Street, Vineyard, Utah.

The purpose of the Public Hearing is to receive public comment regarding proposed amendments to the Vineyard City Consolidated Fee Schedule, including updates and modifications to fees associated with:
-	Parks and Recreation programs and services
-	Vineyard Days events and activities
-	Library services

All interested persons are invited to attend the hearing and provide comment. Written comments may also be submitted to the City Recorder prior to the meeting at robinr@vineyardutah.gov

In compliance with the Americans with Disabilities Act, individuals needing special accommodations (including auxiliary communicative aids and services) should notify the City Recorder at least three (3) business days prior to the hearing. 801-226-1929

I, the undersigned City Recorder for Vineyard, Utah, hereby certify that the foregoing Public Notice as posted at the Vineyard City Office, on the Vineyard City website, and on the Utah Public Notice Website.

Published this 13 day of April 2026.
Robin Bond, Vineyard City Recorder
